author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message: README: add (gqrx-scanner-monitor) name to loopback step

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
README: clarify audio interception section with Virtual Null Sink and volume controls

- Replace 'null sink' terminology with 'Virtual Null Sink ("Gqrx Scanner
  Intercept")' throughout
- Add emphasis on digital decoder use case
- Add 'Volume controls after interception' section documenting source app
  vs gqrx-scanner-monitor volume sliders

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message: Guard VoxAudioReset calls with #ifndef OSX for macOS build

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
pw-loopback: add media.class=Stream/Output/Audio on playback side to enable volume control

The pw-loopback stream (gqrx-scanner-monitor) lacked proper PulseAudio
volume control because its playback node did not have
media.class=Stream/Output/Audio set, causing the PipeWire-PulseAudio
compat layer to ignore volume slider changes.

Fixes: volume slider on gqrx-scanner-monitor had no effect.

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
VOX+timing refactor: wall-clock listen timer, µs precision elapsed, VAD reset

- Replace counter-based listen_time/consecutive_silent with wall-clock
  listen_timer_t (gettimeofday) in WaitUserInputOrDelay
- Merge duplicated VOX detection blocks
- VoxAudioReset() moved from WaitUserInputOrDelay entry to after
  SetFreq() in bookmark and sweep scan loops
- GetTime returns struct timeval (µs precision), DiffTime uses
  gettimeofday — fixes elapsed time second-boundary rounding
- Add VoxAudioReset() helper in vox-audio.c

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Unlimited pw-cat restart, clarify README on audio setup script

- Remove one-shot guard: VoxAudioRestart() is called on every pipe death,
  not just the first. Only popen() failure disables VOX.
- README: clarify that gqrx-scan-setup-audio.sh is optional — by default
  pw-cat captures the default audio monitor (all system audio). Script is
  only needed when other audio sources (music, notifications) would
  falsely trigger VOX.

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Factor open_capture_pipe() out of VoxAudioInit/VoxAudioRestart

- Extract shared popen+pipe-configuration into static open_capture_pipe()
- Both VoxAudioInit() and VoxAudioRestart() call it instead of duplicating
  the popen, setvbuf, fcntl, pipe_dead reset logic
- No behavior change — all 61 tests pass

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Add one-shot pw-cat restart on pipe death

- Extract PW_CAT_COMMAND macro shared between VoxAudioInit() and VoxAudioRestart()
- Add VoxAudioRestart(): pclose() old pipe, popen() new pw-cat, reconfigure
  pipe (unbuffer, non-blocking), re-init VAD state via VoxVADInit()
- Does NOT touch SIGPIPE (already SIG_IGN from init)
- In WaitUserInputOrDelay(): on first !VoxAudioIsAlive(), attempt one
  restart; if it fails (or was already attempted), disable VOX permanently
- All 61 tests pass, zero build warnings

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
BacktrackFrequency rewrite with calibrating GetSignalLevelEx, Debounce hysteresis fix

- GetSignalLevelEx: rewritten with calibrate/fast paths. Calibrate path polls every 50ms, tracks noise -> carrier max, detects settle stability (3 consecutive polls within 1dB), auto-adapts g_settle_time_us via EWMA. Fast path returns max of n_samp rapid reads (used by scan-loop/Debounce).

- BacktrackFrequency: now returns bool with out-params (freq+level). Peak-picker sorts candidates by level descending instead of returning first. Single-candidate accepted only if >=5dB above squelch (BACKTRACK_MIN_MARGIN_DB). Backtrack always runs unconditionally on Missing signal.

- Debounce: applies 3dB hysteresis margin (DEBOUNCE_HYSTERESIS_DB) before declaring signal lost, preventing backtrack oscillation on marginal carriers fluctuating +/-2-3dB.

- AdjustFrequency: replaced usleep(150000) with GetSignalLevelEx(calibrate) at all 6 call sites; reference_level folded into existing settle call.

- Send() guard: returns false if g_socket_dead, preventing writes to known-dead sockets.

- SetFreq retry: added error_retries <= 0 check to avoid indefinite retry.

- -d default changed from 2000 to 2500 ms.

- Mock socket: RESPONSE_QUEUE_MAX 16 -> 128; g_socket_dead reset in mock_socket_reset().

- test_backtrack_frequency_wraparound: fixed mock response count (10->11 per calibrate block, matching 1 initial + 10 polls); removed stale recheck/padding entries. Both backtrack tests updated for new bool API.

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
VAD DC fix, scan algorithm rework, reconnect hardening

SetModulationAndBandwidth (new feature):
  Add modulation-per-bookmark support.  Gqrx bookmarks store the
  demodulator mode as a human-readable string ("Narrow FM") in CSV
  field 4 and the filter bandwidth in field 5.  Previously these
  were ignored and the scanner left the previous demodulator mode
  active when jumping to a new frequency -- making multi-mode
  scanning (e.g. AM airband + NFM repeaters + WFM broadcast)
  unusable.

  Changes:
   - mode_table_entry_t struct + static const mode_table[] in
     gqrx-prot.h mapping 13 Gqrx bookmark descriptions to protocol
     mode IDs (OFF, RAW, AM, AMS, LSB, USB, CWL, CWR, CWU, CW,
     FM, WFM, WFM_ST, WFM_ST_OIRT)
   - SetModulationAndBandwidth() in gqrx-prot.c: looks up the
     mode string, sends "M <id> <bw>\n", checks RPRT 1
   - FREQ struct gains modulation[BUFSIZE] and bandwidth[BUFSIZE]
   - LoadFrequencies() parses bookmark CSV fields 4 and 5 with
     whitespace trimming
   - ScanBookmarkedFrequenciesInRange() calls SetModulationAndBandwidth()
     after SetFreq() for every bookmark hit

VAD:
  - Per-frame DC removal in VoxLpcPitchStrength (static noise false positives)
  - Consecutive pitch threshold 3->2, test_vad_noise2, voice threshold >0.20

Scan algorithm:
  - BacktrackFrequency rewritten to peak-pick over level trace + settle+recheck
  - AdjustFrequency: inclusive bounds, fix second-pass max search
  - carrier_acquired goto path for backtrack reacquisition
  - Saved frequency validation and corrupt-entry reset
  - SaveFreq overflow: zero full array with memset

Reconnect hardening:
  - Socket failure counter (3 strikes -> exit) in WaitUserInputOrDelay
  - g_socket_dead check before every protocol call in scan loops

Housekeeping: remove stray cmocka.h, add noise2.raw + README, fix tests

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Prevent indefinite hang on broken TCP connection after suspend

- Add SO_RCVTIMEO/SO_SNDTIMEO (3s) on the Gqrx socket after connect()
  so read() returns with EAGAIN instead of blocking forever on a
  half-open TCP connection (e.g. after resume from suspend).

- Make Send() and Recv() return false on timeout/error instead of
  calling error() which exits the process.  The protocol commands now
  propagate failure up to the caller.

- In WaitUserInputOrDelay() skip loop iteration when the socket read
  fails, sleeping 100ms before retrying.  This prevents the scanner
  from acting on stale data or hanging on a dead connection.

- Guard setsockopt calls behind #ifndef TESTING_BUILD since mocked
  test sockets do not support SO_RCVTIMEO.

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Fix macOS build: guard opt_max_probe and two-phase VOX code with #ifndef OSX

opt_max_probe and opt_vox are declared inside #ifndef OSX guards but were
referenced without guards in the -l parsing (two references) and in the
two-phase VOX exit logic in WaitUserInputOrDelay(). Wrap those references
in matching #ifndef OSX … #endif so macOS compilation succeeds.

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Add two-phase VOX timing: probe_time:hangup_time for -l

-l now accepts optional colon-separated values: -l [probe_time:]hangup_time
- probe_time (ms): max time to wait for voice on a new carrier
- hangup_time (ms): max silence after voice stops before moving on
- Single value sets both equal (backward compatible)

Implementation:
- opt_max_probe: new global for probe timeout (μs)
- WaitUserInputOrDelay: voice_was_detected flag switches between two phases
- Phase 1 (no voice yet): exit after opt_max_probe of total listen time
- Phase 2 (voice heard): exit after opt_max_listen of consecutive silence

Also fixed bookmark mode: reset skip=false after settle sleep to prevent
slow_scan_cycle (1s) from persisting across subsequent bookmarks.

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Fix audio setup: orphaned-stream recovery, pw-cat JSON properties

gqrx-scan-setup-audio-pw.sh:
  - Add connect_stream_to_sink() helper for orphaned output ports
  - Fix find_default_sink awk logic (emit on node start, not blank line)
  - Handle orphaned streams in attach (fallback to default sink)
  - Cleanup: kill orphaned pw-cat processes, recover stray streams

src/vox-audio.c:
  - Switch pw-cat --properties from key=value to JSON -P format
  - Add node.name, application.name, application.id metadata

message:
Merge pull request #34 from neural75/feat/vox-audio

Voice Activity Detection (VOX) with PipeWire audio capture

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message: Add POST_BUILD copy of binary to bin/ for CI verify step

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message: Fix CI: add missing <setjmp.h> in vad_test.c, guard opt_vox usage on macOS

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message: Add --vox option description

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Fix DiffTime sprintf buffer overflow warning

Increase days buffer from 10 to 32 bytes and use snprintf instead of
sprintf to silence gcc -Wformat-overflow/-Wformat-truncation.

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message: Merge branch 'feat/delay-calibration' into feat/vox-audio

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
vox: suppress receiver hiss false positives with pitch-sustain VAD

- Pitch scoring requires 3+ consecutive frames > 0.35 (isolated hiss
  resets the counter, real voice sustains across many frames)
- Raised ZC thresholds (0.30->0.35 pitch, 0.50->0.55 ZC)
- Reduced hangover (6->2 frames)
- Fixed max_lag clamping (N-32) in VoxLpcPitchStrength
- consecutive_silent counter in caller for proper -l N timing
- VoxAudioHasSignal drains pipe in batches for frame-granular VAD
- Added hiss_low/high, voice_with_3s_pause test fixtures
- All 60 tests pass

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Refactor VOX into modular LPC analysis and pitch-based VAD modules

Extract LPC functions (autocorrelation, Levinson-Durbin, LSF conversion,
prediction gain, zero-crossings, pitch strength) into shared vox-lpc.h/c.
Replace the energy-threshold and G.729B VAD with a simple pitch-based
VAD in vox-vad.h/c that uses only amplitude-invariant features:
pitch periodicity (counts double), ZC extremes, and band-energy
differential.  Add VoxAudioHexDump() helper for standalone debug use.
Wire --vox flag into gqrx-scan.c/h.  Update CMake build files and
default to Release with make debug/release convenience targets.

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Fix pw-cat monitor capture with stream.capture.sink property

pw-cat --record needs --properties='stream.capture.sink=true
target.object=<name>' to capture from a sink's monitor ports;
-d <name>.monitor only works with PulseAudio compat layer (pacat).

Also:
- Fix cmake_uninstall.cmake.in deprecation (exec_program -> execute_process)
- Fix CMake install of audio script (target was installed as broken symlink)
- vox-audio.c: pw-cat primary, _PA reference functions for debug

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
VOX audio detection module + PipeWire audio setup script + source reorg

- New vox-audio module (src/vox-audio.c/h):
  - Primary: VoxAudioInit/Shutdown using pw-cat (PipeWire-native)
  - Reference: VoxAudioInit_PA/Shutdown_PA using pacat (PulseAudio, for debug)
  - SIGPIPE ignored during capture via sigaction save/restore
  - Probes for tool availability before popen with actionable errors

- Audio interception scripts:
  - PipeWire-native: gqrx-scan-setup-audio-pw.sh (pw-cli/pw-link/pw-loopback)
  - PulseAudio: gqrx-scan-setup-audio-pa.sh (pactl, kept for reference)
  - Symlink: gqrx-scan-setup-audio.sh -> -pw.sh

- Source reorganization:
  - Moved all C sources/headers to src/ (gqrx-scan, gqrx-prot, vox-audio)
  - Updated CMakeLists.txt include/source paths
  - Fixed test include paths (unit_tests, sweep_test, mocks)

- AGENTS.md: updated Architecture section, added Code Comments guide

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message: Revert to a more simple default, all tentative to make it smarter failed

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
feat: adaptive delay calibration with poll-and-flush settling detection

Replaces the blind usleep with an adaptive poll loop that:
- Polls GetSignalLevel every 50ms up to 350ms
- Ignores above-squelch readings until the level first drops below
  squelch (seen_below guard), filtering stale bleeds from previous freq
- Catches real signals early when level transitions above squelch after
  the flush, before the timeout fires
- Falls back to GetSignalLevelEx(5) on timeout (350ms)
- opt_speed=0 enables adaptive mode; any positive -x value uses fixed delay
- -v debug output shows per-poll levels and exit reason
- Default changed from 250ms blind wait to adaptive (350ms max)

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Remove squelch-delta-auto, noise_floor, sweep Frequencies alloc; add Debounce/Backtrack unit tests

Production:
- Remove -q/--squelch_delta CLI option, opt_squelch_delta,
  opt_squelch_delta_auto_enable globals, and all SetSquelchLevel calls
  that modified receiver squelch on hit. The option tried to adjust the
  Gqrx squelch dynamically based on noise floor, but the implementation
  was fragile — its auto-mode carried complexity through both scan paths
  without clear benefit, and mid-scan squelch changes could confuse the
  user or cause visual glitches in Gqrx's UI. More fundamentally,
  setting the squelch level is the receiver operator's responsibility
  (separation of concerns): this program should detect signals against
  whatever squelch the user has configured, not second-guess it.
  Removing the option simplifies the code and eliminates the noise_floor
  tracking that existed only to support it.
- Remove noise_floor field from FREQ struct, its init/averaging in both
  scan modes, and associated commented-out debug prints
- Sweep mode no longer allocates the Frequencies array (previously was
  unconditional; only used to store per-bin noise_floor for the removed
  squelch-delta feature). The malloc is now inside
  if (opt_scan_mode == bookmark) where it belongs.
- Remove squelch_delta parameter from ScanFrequenciesInRange and
  ScanBookmarkedFrequenciesInRange signatures
- Fix pre-existing bug: fclose(bookmarksfd) on uninitialized pointer in
  sweep mode (initialize to NULL, guard fclose)

Tests:
- Add 4 socket-mocked unit tests (Linux, HAVE_WRAP_SOCKET_MOCKS):
  - test_debounce_signal_persists (above squelch -> true)
  - test_debounce_signal_lost (below squelch -> false)
  - test_backtrack_frequency_wraparound (wraps on underflow, finds signal)
  - test_backtrack_frequency_not_found (exhausts intervals, no signal)
- Remove sweep test's manual Frequencies allocation (no longer needed)
- Remove squelch_delta args from sweep/bookmark test calls
- Add descr/tag lookup to bookmark test diagnostic output
- Reduce n_loops from 2 to 1 for bookmark tests (cleaner 1:1 mapping)
- Update README.md: remove -q docs

All 53 tests pass (46 unit + 7 sweep).

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message:
Add bookmark scan mode integration tests + parse_hit_report robustness fix

- parse_hit_report: advance by searching for next 'Freq:' instead of
  strchr(tag, '\n'), correctly handling multi-line squelch_delta_auto output
- validate_hit_line(): verify stdout line format — 'active' with correct
  descr brackets and 'Level: -XX.XX/-XX.XX'
- Existing sweep tests now call validate_hit_line for each expected hit
- Add TESTING_BUILD exit hook for ScanBookmarkedFrequenciesInRange
  (g_testing_max_bookmark_loops)
- Declare ScanBookmarkedFrequenciesInRange in header
- 3 bookmark integration tests: all_hit, some_hit, all_noise
- 3 new profiles: test/profiles/bookmark_*.txt

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message: Rename nearby_signals profile to two_nearby_signal

author: Piergiorgio Trubini
message: Add 3-peak sweep test (well-separated carriers, 1 MHz range)
